rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF AMIR SIMBULAN NASSER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues need to be cleared. a. Need explanation "funds advanced to Alex Ignacio" in the amount of $60,000 in total. If this was a personal loan. Also, if Alex Ignacio is related to the party. b. Need allegation who Alex Ignacio is and why he advanced the funds of $40K to this estate. c. Need explanation re: $1,550 paid by Ligaya for "vacant unit" on 9/4/2020. d. Need explanation re: $13,770 paid by Ligaya for rental income on 12/30/2020 for her sister e. Need further allegation the payment $1,320.49 for "repair" to 1688 11th Ave. the nature of repair not explained. f. Need statement disclosing any family or affiliate relationship between the fiduciary and anyone hired by the fiduciary, including Higgins, Alex Ignacio and other names appearing multiple times on the schedules. PC 1064(a)(4). g. Petition states the former attorney for the minor's information while the minor is represented by Ms. Harrington now. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAYMUNDO LUNA TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/02/2023, as the following examiner notes (e-mailed to counsel on 04/19/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No courtesy copies have been received. 2) No proposed order has been received. 3) No additional bond has been filed. 4) Please provide a proof of recording of the transfer of 50% ownership of the 121 Lisbon Street property to the trust. 5) The security deposits received and refunded during the account period must be allocated to principal. See Probate Code section 16356. 6) The security deposits remaining at the end of the account period must also be listed on the schedule of liabilities. 7) The legal fees for eviction must be allocated one-half to principal and one-half to income. See sections 16370(b) and 16371(a)(1). 8) Please explain why the bond premiums should not be allocated to income. See section 16370(c) & (d). 9) Schedule C-1b reports repayment of $114,568.66 to Andres Luna from the beneficiary's personal account, but according to the petitioner's declaration filed on 5/19/22, the repayment was made from the trust. 10) Should the correct total costs be $1,729.24 + $215.21 + $460.33 = $2,404.78? Note: An appearance may be required on 08/02/2023 for the Court to consider whether to take this petition off calendar, whether to suspend the trustee, and/or whether to issue an order to show cause re monetary sanctions, if no satisfactory progress is shown to clear the outstanding notes.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAY L. BLOOM TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/10/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) For the 6th accounting, a total of $5,360.08 was allowed for that one-year accounting period, based on 1% of the FMV of assets on hand at the end of that accounting period. Now, fees are based on actual time spent, but includes both attorney and trustee time spent on the 6th accounting. (2) Additionally, five hours were billed by the attorney to prepare this accounting, as well as numerous hours billed by the trustee to calculate the income distribution, but another 69.30 hours are being requested, albeit at a lower hourly rate, to input receipts and disbursements into a ledger..? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 6/28/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GUILLERMO COBAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Statement Of Interest And Petition For Order Approving Assignment Of Interests In Estate And Authorizing Distribution Directly To Celtic Research
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/03/2023. 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 3) Direct notice must be given to the personal representative. See CRC 7.51(a). 4) Direct notice must be given to Bridge Financial Funding LLC as a person who requested special notice, in addition to a separate copy given to its counsel. 5) Copies of the petition and supplemental papers must be served on all persons who requested special notice. Item 5 of the proof of service attached to the DE-120 form must be checked. 6) The petition must be signed by an authorized representative of Celtic Research. See CRC 7.103(a). 7) Is the relief sought by this petition premature, given that no accountings have been filed? See Estate of Ramsey (1951) 107 Cal.App.2d 372, 375 [Clearly it is within the discretion of the court to conduct such hearings in advance as well as at the time of the hearing of a petition for distribution. However, in the present case, the hearing was had prior to the filing of any account by the executor, although it was necessary for the court to know the value of the interests of appellants in the residuary estate in order to determine whether the sum they had received for their assignments was fair or grossly inadequate. We think that where interests have been assigned outright the court might properly refuse to conduct a hearing under section 1020.1 in advance of a hearing upon an account of the executor or administrator.]; see also Estate of Boyd (1979) 98 Cal.App.3d 125, 134 ["The import of the holding in Ramsey is that the court must have some reliable foundation on which to base its evaluation of the property. Where no executor's account had been filed, the court "might properly refuse to conduct a hearing under section 1020.1."]

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (No courtesy copy of the second supplement filed on 6/2/23 has been received yet by the assigned examiner as of 6/7/23.) The second supplement does not appear to have been signed/verified by the petitioner. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. (A proof of service attached to a pleading is never appropriate in Probate proceedings if notice is required. Notice is not required for a supplement to a pleading. [CRC 7.3 and 7.53(b).])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALVIN G. WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 10/29/2020. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. Status of closing the estate will be set for 6/24/24. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before 6/24/24, no appearance will be required on that date.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KEITH AARON COLLINS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/2/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) No proof of publication of notice. (4) No Acknowledgement of Receipt of Duties and Liabilities. (5) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/24/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BETTY SUEY HAI YEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/3/2023. The following procedural issues need to be cleared two weeks prior: (1) Petition to complete #2.d.(2) re: the amount of bond be fixed. (2) Petition #3.d.(2)-(5) to complete re: estimate the value (3) Need Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number [JC Form DE-147S] to be filed. LRSF 14.18.E. (4) Duties of Liabilities form to include the proposed fiduciary's telephone number. (5) Written declination by four heirs - Linda, Lily, Nany and Lotus. Otherwise, Petition to indicate the reason if no written document is on file. LRSF 14.24 (6) Proof of notice of direction for remote appearances to all persons entitled. LRSF 14.1.D.6) (7) Proposed Order to be submitted prior to the hearing. LRSF 14.7.A.
